popularStory.description: Once upon a time, a couple named Hezo and Ida, from a West Flanders town called Wervik or Wervicq, had three beautiful daughters named Helwigis, Jutta, and Giselindis. These three girls were walking i …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Adèle de France於1009年5月3日出生在法國米迪-列內斯豪-加洛圖勞斯時，她的父親Robert II le Pieux roi de France37歲，母親Constance d'Arles37歲。她於1028年與Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut在法國阿希迪瑟 德 芭黎絲結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1079年1月8日在比利時維斯吉福拉安德倫易也享年69歲，埋葬在比利時維斯吉福拉安德倫梅森。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Adèle de France于1009年5月3日出生在法国米迪-列內斯豪-加洛圖勞斯时，她的父亲Robert II le Pieux roi de France37岁，母亲Constance d'Arles37岁。她于1028年与Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut在法国阿希迪瑟 德 芭黎絲结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1079年1月8日在比利时維斯吉福拉安德倫易也享年69岁，埋葬在比利时維斯吉福拉安德倫梅森。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: France
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: ethnic name for someone from France (see 2 below).

French: habitational name given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris); or, in southern France, an ethnic name for someone from what is now the northern half of the country, where the langue d'oïl (Old French as distinct from Occitan) was spoken. Compare   Defrance   and   Lafrance  .
Czech (Franče): from a pet form of František, Latin Franciscus (see Francis ).

variants.name: Defrance
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Henri, Jean-Paul.

French (mainly northern): habitational name, with fused preposition de ‘from’, given to someone from France (at the time when the name only applied to those lands belonging to the French king, covering only a small part of its current territory) or from Île-de-France (i.e. from a region centered on Paris). Compare France .

nameMeanings.name: Adèle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From French Adèle , an ancient name popular in medieval Europe because of the fame of a 7th-century saint, a daughter of the Frankish king Dagobert II. It is of Germanic origin, from adal ‘noble’ (a short form of a two-element name such as Adelheid; see Adelaide ). It was the name of William the Conqueror's youngest daughter ( c.1062–1137 ), who became the wife of Stephen of Blois. However, it died out in England in the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, being the name of a character in Johann Strauss's opera Die Fledermaus. Its popularity was further reinforced in the 1930s as the name of a character in the novels of Dornford Yates .

variants.name: Adel
meanings: From French Adèle , an ancient name popular in medieval Europe because of the fame of a 7th-century saint, a daughter of the Frankish king Dagobert II. It is of Germanic origin, from adal ‘noble’ (a short form of a two-element name such as Adelheid; see Adelaide ). It was the name of William the Conqueror's youngest daughter ( c.1062–1137 ), who became the wife of Stephen of Blois. However, it died out in England in the later Middle Ages. It is of Germanic origin, from adal ‘noble’ (a short form of a two-element name such as Adelheid; see Adelaide ). It was the name of William the Conqueror's youngest daughter ( c.1062–1137 ), who became the wife of Stephen of Blois. However, it died out in England in the later Middle Ages. It was revived in the 19th century, being the name of a character in Johann Strauss's opera Die Fledermaus. Its popularity was further reinforced in the 1930s as the name of a character in the novels of Dornford Yates .

en.lifeSummary: When Adèle de France was born on 3 May 1009, in Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, France, her father, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, was 37 and her mother, Constance d'Arles, was 37. She married Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut in 1028, in Archdiocese of Paris, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 8 January 1079, in Ieper, West Flanders, Belgium, at the age of 69, and was buried in Mesen, West Flanders, Belgium.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Adèle de France est née le 3 mai 1009, à Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, France, son père, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Constance d'Arles, avait 37 ans. Elle a épousé Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut en 1028, à Archidiocèse de Paris, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 8 janvier 1079, à Ypres, Flandre occidentale, Belgique, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Messines, Flandre occidentale, Belgique.

de.lifeSummary: Als Adèle de France am 3. Mai 1009 in Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert II le Pieux roi de France 37 und ihre Mutter Constance d'Arles 37. Sie hat 1028 in Archidiocèse de Paris, Frankreich Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 8. Januar 1079 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Ieper, West-Flandern, Belgien und wurde in Mesen, West-Flandern, Belgien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Adèle de France è nata il il 3 maggio 1009 nel Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, Francia, sua padre, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, aveva 37 anni e sua madre, Constance d'Arles, aveva 37 anni. Lei sposato Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut nel 1028, nel Archidiocèse de Paris, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 8 gennaio 1079, nel Ieper, Fiandra Occidentale, Belgio, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a Mesen, Fiandra Occidentale, Belgio.

ja.lifeSummary: Adèle de Franceが1009年5月3日にフランスミディ-プイルeンeスハウテ-ガーオントウロウゼで生まれた時，父Robert II le Pieux roi de Franceは37歳，母Constance d'Arlesは37歳であった。1028年にフランスアーチディオクeゼ ド パーイスでBaudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainautと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1079年1月8日にベルギーウエスト-vラアンデレンイエペルで69歳で死去し，ベルギーウエスト-vラアンデレンメセンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Adèle de France(이)가 1009년 5월 3일 프랑스 미디-p이ee스 하테-가니 토우루스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert II le Pieux roi de France(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Constance d'Arles(은)는 37세였다. 그녀는 1028년 프랑스 아치디오ce스 데 파리스에서 Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1079년 1월 8일 벨기에 웨스트-블라아더 이퍼에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 벨기에 웨스트-블라아더 메센에 묻혔다.

mn.3 May 1009–8 January 1079: 1009 оны Тавдугаар сарын 3–1079 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 8

pt.3 May 1009–8 January 1079: 3 de maio de 1009–8 de janeiro de 1079
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Adèle de France nasceu em 3 de maio de 1009, em Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Midi-Pyrénées, França, seu pai, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Constance d'Arles, tinha 37 anos. Ela casou-se com Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut em 1028, em Archidiocèse de Paris, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 8 de janeiro de 1079, em Ieper, Flandres Ocidental, Bélgica, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Mesen, Flandres Ocidental, Bélgica.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Adèle de France родилась 3 мая 1009 г. в местности тоулоус, хауте-гаронн, миди-пирeнeесь, Франция, её отцу по имени Robert II le Pieux roi de France было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Constance d'Arles, 37. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut в 1028 г. в местности арчидиокeс де пэрис, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 8 января 1079 г. в местности иепир, вест-влаандирен, Бельгия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности месен, вест-влаандирен, Бельгия.

es.3 May 1009–8 January 1079: 3 de mayo de 1009–8 de enero de 1079
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Adèle de France nació el 3 de mayo de 1009, en Toulouse, Haute-Garonne, Mediodía-Pirineos, Francia, su papá, Robert II le Pieux roi de France, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Constance d'Arles, tenía 37 años. Se casó con Baudouin V comte de Flandre et de Hainaut en 1028, en Archidiocèse de Paris, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 8 de enero de 1079, en Ieper, Flandes Occidental, Bélgica, a la edad de 69 años, y fue sepultada en Mesen, Flandes Occidental, Bélgica.
